questions.question: What is the definition of a linear transformation?
keyPoints: A linear transformation is a way of transforming vectors from one vector space to another
It preserves the operations of vector addition and scalar multiplication

questions.question: What is the kernel of a linear transformation?
keyPoints: The kernel of a linear transformation is the set of all vectors v such that T(v) = 0
It is also known as the null space of the linear transformation

questions.question: What is the range of a linear transformation?
keyPoints: The range of a linear transformation is the set of all vectors w such that w = T(v) for some v
It is also known as the image of the linear transformation

questions.question: What is the rank-nullity theorem?
keyPoints: The rank-nullity theorem states that for a linear transformation T, the rank of T plus the nullity of T is equal to the dimension of the domain
It is a fundamental theorem in linear algebra

questions.question: What is the matrix representation of a linear transformation?
keyPoints: The matrix representation of a linear transformation is a matrix A such that T(v) = Av for all v
It is a way of representing a linear transformation using a matrix

questions.question: Prove that the composition of two linear transformations is a linear transformation
keyPoints: The composition of two linear transformations is a linear transformation
It preserves the operations of vector addition and scalar multiplication

questions.question: Find the kernel and range of a given linear transformation
keyPoints: The kernel of a linear transformation is the set of all vectors v such that T(v) = 0
The range of a linear transformation is the set of all vectors w such that w = T(v) for some v

questions.question: Apply the rank-nullity theorem to a given linear transformation
keyPoints: The rank-nullity theorem states that for a linear transformation T, the rank of T plus the nullity of T is equal to the dimension of the domain
It is a fundamental theorem in linear algebra
